meaning: An informal way to admit a mistake
example: Oh, my bad! I thought the meeting was at 3, not 2

meaning: It's okay, don't worry about it
example: You forgot the milk? No worries, I'll grab some later

meaning: I agree, I want to do that
example: Pizza for dinner? I'm totally down!

spanish: es obvio, no hay que pensarlo
meaning: Something so obvious it requires no thought
example: Free concert tickets? That's a no-brainer!

topic: to be on the same page
meaning: To have the same understanding about something
example: Let's make sure we're on the same page before the presentation

spanish: en resumen, para no hacer el cuento largo
meaning: To summarize something without all the details
example: Long story short, I missed my flight and had to take a bus

spanish: no es tan difícil, no es ciencia espacial
meaning: It's not as complicated as you think
example: Come on, it's not rocket science — just follow the recipe

meaning: To wait until the next day before making a decision
example: It's a big decision — let me sleep on it

meaning: All the time, constantly, without stopping
example: This store is open 24/7 — you can go anytime

meaning: A warning or advance notice about something
example: Heads up — the boss is in a bad mood today

meaning: As quickly as you can
example: Please send me that report ASAP

meaning: Used to introduce a new topic in conversation
example: By the way, did you hear about the new restaurant?

spanish: tener ganas de, estar dispuesto a
meaning: To be willing or interested in doing something
example: Are you up for grabbing coffee after work?

meaning: I need or would benefit from something
example: I could use a vacation right about now

meaning: I understand, that's logical
example: Oh, that makes sense! Thanks for explaining

meaning: I'm fine OR no thank you (depends on context)
example: Want more coffee? — I'm good, thanks!

meaning: Seriously, truly, not joking
example: You got the job? For real?! That's amazing!

meaning: To solve or understand something
example: Don't worry, we'll figure it out together

meaning: What's the hidden problem or disadvantage?
example: Free vacation? What's the catch?

meaning: You did something perfectly
example: Your presentation was amazing — you nailed it!

meaning: Keep going, don't give up during a difficult time
example: I know the exam is hard, but hang in there!

meaning: To relax and accept what happens without resisting
example: I don't have a plan — I'm just going with the flow

topic: to be a piece of cake
spanish: ser pan comido, ser muy fácil
meaning: Something that is very easy to do
example: The test was a piece of cake — I finished in 10 minutes

meaning: To have an amazing, fun time
example: We had a blast at the beach party last night

spanish: no es lo mío, no me gusta
meaning: Not something I enjoy or prefer
example: Horror movies are not my cup of tea — I prefer comedies

meaning: Very rarely, almost never
example: I only eat fast food once in a blue moon

meaning: Contact me, get in touch
example: If you're free this weekend, hit me up!

spanish: no voy a mentir, siendo honesto
meaning: Being honest, telling the truth
example: I'm not gonna lie, that movie was terrible

meaning: That's reasonable, I accept your point
example: You don't want to go out? Fair enough, let's stay in

spanish: es lo que es, no se puede cambiar
meaning: Accepting a situation that can't be changed
example: The flight is delayed 3 hours — it is what it is
